subject: Nikola Tesla - Complete Projects, Inventions, Patents & Documents
research_scope: All known projects, experiments, patents (300+ worldwide), archived documents, and writings

ac_electrical_system.name: Alternating Current (AC) Electrical System
ac_electrical_system.description: Modern electrical power distribution system using alternating current
ac_electrical_system.documents: The Inventions, Researches and Writings of Nikola Tesla (1894)

induction_motor.name: Polyphase Induction Motor with Rotating Magnetic Field
induction_motor.description: First practical AC motor using rotating magnetic fields. Seven U.S. patents granted in 1888.
key_patents: Two-phase and three-phase motor and generator patents
induction_motor.significance: Enabled practical AC power applications in machinery

tesla_coil.name: Tesla Coil Transformer
tesla_coil.description: Electrical transformer that steps up voltage to extremely high levels. Uses insulating oil baths.
tesla_coil.specifications: Achieved frequencies of 20,000 cycles per second at extremely high voltages
tesla_coil.patents: US568176A and US568179A (1896) - High frequency apparatus
tesla_coil.current_use: Still used in radio technology and scientific experimentation

wireless_power_transmission.name: Wireless Power Transmission System
wireless_power_transmission.description: System to transmit electrical energy without wires over long distances
wireless_power_transmission.patents: US649621A (1900) - Apparatus for transmission of electrical energy

wardenclyffe_tower.status: Partially completed; abandoned due to funding
wardenclyffe_tower.purpose: Worldwide wireless transmission of power and information
wardenclyffe_tower.current_status: Site now home to Tesla Science Center at Wardenclyffe (museum)

remote_control_technology.name: Teleautomatic Boat (Remote Control)
remote_control_technology.description: Boat guided by remote control using wireless signals, early form of radio control
remote_control_technology.demonstration: Public demonstration at Madison Square Garden, New York

colorado_springs_experiments.name: Colorado Springs High-Voltage Research Laboratory
colorado_springs_experiments.description: Experimental laboratory for high-voltage and high-frequency experiments
colorado_springs_experiments.host: Free location and electricity provided by friend Leonard Curtis
colorado_springs_experiments.reason_for_location: Laboratory on Houston Street, NYC no longer suitable for extreme high-voltage work
colorado_springs_experiments.documentation: Comprehensive laboratory notes and technical records

x_ray_research.name: X-Ray Experimentation (Shadowgraph)
x_ray_research.description: Tesla experimented with shadowgraphs similar to X-ray technology before Röntgen's discovery (1895)
x_ray_research.significance: Early work in radiation and high-frequency phenomena
x_ray_research.relationship_to_röntgen: Parallel research to Wilhelm Röntgen's X-ray discovery

neon_and_phosphorescent_lighting.name: Neon and Phosphorescent Light Development
neon_and_phosphorescent_lighting.description: High-frequency power experiments led to neon light creation and phosphorescent lighting
neon_and_phosphorescent_lighting.significance: Foundation of neon sign industry and modern lighting technology

particle_beam_weapon.name: Particle Beam Weapon (Teleforce / 'Death Ray')
particle_beam_weapon.status: Theoretical and experimental; never fully built
particle_beam_weapon.description: Military weapon accelerating mercury particles at 48x speed of sound through vacuum chamber
particle_beam_weapon.tesla_intent: Called it a 'peace beam' rather than 'death ray' - intended for defense/deterrence
government_interest: United States - Tesla offered; no significant interest
Soviet Union - Only country to show interest; conducted partial tests (1939)
particle_beam_weapon.declassified_documents: CIA documents reveal secret history of this project

bladeless_turbine.name: Tesla Turbine (Bladeless Rotor)
bladeless_turbine.description: Turbine design using rotating disk instead of blades
bladeless_turbine.advantage: High efficiency, simple construction, no moving parts in traditional sense

earthquake_machine.name: Mechanical Oscillator (Earthquake Machine)
earthquake_machine.description: Mechanical device that vibrates at specific frequencies; claimed ability to induce vibrations in structures
earthquake_machine.famous_incident: Tesla reportedly used device in NYC lab; caused vibrations felt in surrounding buildings
earthquake_machine.status_verification: Some claims legendary; core device documented

thought_camera.name: Thought Photography Device
thought_camera.description: Tesla claimed to have designed device capable of projecting thoughts as images on screen
thought_camera.basis: Theoretical extensions of electromagnetic and consciousness theories
thought_camera.scientific_status: Not supported by verified experimental evidence

cosmic_ray_motor.name: Motor Powered by Cosmic Rays
cosmic_ray_motor.description: Theoretical motor design claimed to run on cosmic radiation energy

artificial_intelligence_concepts.name: Early Concepts of Wireless Automata and Remote Control
artificial_intelligence_concepts.description: Theoretical concepts of self-operating machines guided by wireless signals

nikola_tesla_museum_belgrade.holdings: Personal and original legacy collection (brought to museum 1952)

library_of_congress.citation_format: Reel number, Nikola Tesla Correspondence, Manuscript Division, Library of Congress

content: Transmission of electric energy without wires

internet_archive.related: The Inventions, Researches and Writings of Nikola Tesla (1894)

my_inventions_autobiography.title: My Inventions: The Autobiography of Nikola Tesla
my_inventions_autobiography.content: Tesla's own account of life, inventions, and discoveries

the_fantastic_inventions.title: The Fantastic Inventions of Nikola Tesla
the_fantastic_inventions.content: Collection of Tesla's writings and articles

inventions_researches_writings_1894.title: The Inventions, Researches and Writings of Nikola Tesla
inventions_researches_writings_1894.focus: Polyphase currents and high potential lighting

tesla_said.title: Tesla Said: Collected Articles and Statements by Nikola Tesla
tesla_said.content: Comprehensive single volume of Tesla's writings

problem_of_increasing_human_energy.title: The Problem of Increasing Human Energy

experiments_with_alternate_currents.title: Experiments with Alternate Currents of High Potential and High Frequency
experiments_with_alternate_currents.publication: In book: Inventions, Researches and Writings of Nikola Tesla

tesla_collection_com.description: Most comprehensive collection of Nikola Tesla material

tesla_science_center_wardenclyffe.content: Historical site, museum, 3D inventions virtual tours

nikola_tesla_museum_digital.content: 3D representations of Tesla's inventions, archive access

free_online_access: Internet Archive (archive.org) - Tesla papers and writings
Library of Congress - Some correspondence (by appointment)
paid_access: Museum archives - Certified copies of originals
in_person_access: Tesla Museum, Belgrade - Original papers and artifacts
Library of Congress - Manuscript Division (by appointment)

research_summary.total_projects_documented: 15+ major projects with hundreds of minor experiments
research_summary.verified_projects: AC system, induction motor, Tesla coil, wireless transmission, remote control, Colorado Springs work, lighting research, turbine designs
research_summary.theoretical_projects: Thought camera, cosmic ray motor, some aspects of particle beam
research_summary.legendary_projects: Earthquake machine (partial evidence), Tesla tower full capabilities (theoretical)

methodology_notes.confidence_high: AC system, motor, coil, wireless transmission, patents, Colorado Springs, neon/phosphorescent lighting
methodology_notes.confidence_medium: Earthquake machine (some evidence), remote control details
methodology_notes.confidence_low: Thought camera (no evidence), cosmic ray motor (theoretical only)
methodology_notes.documents_confirmed: All major documents verified accessible through institutions listed
